body:
Closes #1843.

## Summary

- Render Claude and Codex image outputs inline in `--interactive-mode` PR comments instead of posting base64 blobs.
- Store generated/read images in a hidden custom Git ref, `refs/hive-mind-media/pr-<number>`, using Git Data API blob/tree/commit/ref updates.
- Embed commit-SHA `?raw=true` blob URLs, so the PR branch is untouched and no branch, tag, or GitHub Contents API upload is created.
- Redact image base64 from raw JSON sections; when upload is disabled or fails, fall back to a compact image-metadata note.
- Keep `--interactive-mode` support explicit for Claude and Codex; tests verify other configured tools remain unsupported.
- Add the issue case study under `docs/case-studies/issue-1843`.

## Reproduction

Before this change, a Claude/Codex image result in interactive mode produced a PR comment containing the base64 payload in a fenced block and again in Raw JSON. GitHub did not render the image inline.

The focused image tests reproduce that by driving the Claude tool-result and Codex MCP-result handlers with mocked image payloads and asserting the posted body contains no raw base64.

## Verification

- `node tests/test-interactive-mode-images.mjs` (32 passed)
- `node tests/test-interactive-mode.mjs` (111 passed)
- `node experiments/verify-image-url-survives-sanitizer.mjs`
- `npm run format:check`
- `npm run lint`
- `bash scripts/check-file-line-limits.sh`
- `npm run check:duplication`
- `node --check src/solve.mjs && node --check src/hive.mjs && bash scripts/check-mjs-syntax.sh`
- `npm test` (all 224 selected test files passed)

*Monitored by hive-mind with --auto-restart-until-mergeable flag*
Is there any other way to use GitHub Artifacts? Linked to pull requests? Actions and so on? Repository wide? Any other ways to store images without introducing a branch?

🤖 **AI Work Session Started**

Starting automated work session at 2026-05-30T16:42:15.885Z

The PR has been converted to draft mode while work is in progress.

_This comment marks the beginning of an AI work session. Please wait for the session to finish, and provide your feedback._

Good question — I researched all of these and **verified the viable ones with a live experiment** against this repo (creating throwaway refs via the Git Data API, fetching the resulting URLs, then deleting the refs). Script + raw log: `experiments/storage-probe.sh` / `experiments/storage-probe.log`; full write-up in `docs/case-studies/issue-1843/external/research-notes.md` (Finding 6).

### Short answer
Yes — images can be stored **without any branch** (and without any tag). The catch behind every option is the same: GitHub only renders an image in a comment if the bytes are reachable at an `https://` URL it will proxy through Camo, and a git object is only servable if it's kept alive by **some** ref. So the question is really *which kind of ref* — a branch is just one of three.

### What does NOT work
- **GitHub Actions artifacts** — cannot be embedded inline. They're stored as **zip archives**, their download URLs are **authenticated, signed and expiring** (90‑day default retention), and they can only be **created from inside a workflow run** — there's no PAT‑usable upload endpoint and no stable image URL GitHub will render. ❌
- **PR "attachments"** (`github.com/user-attachments/assets/…`) — the uploader is **cookie/session‑gated and rejects PATs (HTTP 422)**, so it can't be driven headlessly. ❌ (same blocker already documented in the case study)
- **Gists** — token‑creatable, but owned by the **token's user account**, not the repo; poor binary support and inconsistent private‑content access. ❌
- **Release assets** — token‑uploadable, but **require a tag**, pollute the Releases UI, and don't reliably render inline for private repos. ⚠️

### What DOES work (token‑only, renders inline, public + private) — verified
| Approach | Embed URL | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Git tag** `refs/tags/…` | `…/blob/<tag>/<path>?raw=true` | **HTTP 200 `image/png`** ✅ |
| **Custom ref** `refs/hive-mind-media/…` (no branch, no tag) | `…/blob/<commit-sha>/<path>?raw=true` | **HTTP 200 `image/png`** ✅ |

Both reuse the **exact Git Data API flow already in the code** (blob → tree → parentless commit → create ref). The only differences from the current orphan‑branch approach:
- the ref *kind* (`refs/tags/*` or a custom `refs/hive-mind-media/*` namespace instead of `refs/heads/*`), and
- for the custom‑ref option, embedding by **commit SHA** instead of ref name (GitHub's friendly `/blob/<name>/` URLs resolve only `heads/*` and `tags/*`, but a **commit SHA resolves regardless of the namespace** that keeps it alive — which is why a custom ref renders).

### Recommendation
A **custom ref namespace (`refs/hive-mind-media/*`) embedded via the commit‑SHA `?raw=true` URL** is the cleanest "no branch" answer: it keeps the bytes alive, needs no new credentials or services, renders for public **and** private repos, and is **invisible in every GitHub UI list** (branch dropdown, PR base picker, tags/releases) — so it's never a stray merge target. A **git tag** is the simpler alternative (friendly URL, ~one‑line change) but shows up under Tags/Releases.

This is an architectural choice, so I'd like your call before I rewrite the storage layer: **(a) custom ref** (recommended, fully hidden), **(b) git tag** (simplest), or **(c) keep the orphan branch**? I'll implement the option you pick in this PR.

## 🚨 Solution Draft Failed
The automated solution draft encountered an error:
```
CLAUDE execution failed
```

### 🤖 **Models used:**
- Tool: Anthropic Claude Code
- Requested: `opus`
- **Model: Claude Opus 4.8** (`claude-opus-4-8`)

### 📎 **Failure log uploaded as Gist** (2221KB)

---
*Now working session is ended, feel free to review and add any feedback on the solution draft.*

```
Custom ref refs/hive-mind-media/… (no branch, no tag)
```

Lets focus on this by default, and double check that everything will work as expected for all `--tool`s with `--interactive-mode` support.

We need to ensure all changes are correct, consistent, validated, tested, documented, logged and fully meet each and all discussed requirements in deepest and widest possible sense (check issue description and all comments in issue and in pull request, make sure each and every requirement listed before actually checking if they were addressed). Nothing should be defered or delayed. The scope is the entire repository, every change in one place should also be applied in all places in the codebase and docs. Ensure all CI/CD checks pass.

Please plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.

🤖 **AI Work Session Started**

commits.messageBody:
Adding .gitkeep for PR creation (default mode).
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1843

Compile collected issue/PR data and deep analysis under
docs/case-studies/issue-1843: requirements list, external research into
GitHub image-hosting options (data: URIs are stripped; attachments API is
cookie-gated; Contents-API/branch + ?raw=true blob URL is the headless-viable
path), prior art (gh-attach), verified image payload shapes, root cause in
handleToolResult, and the solution plan.

Refs #1843
commits.messageHeadline: docs: add issue-1843 case study (image display in interactive mode)
feat(interactive-mode): display images the AI reads/writes inline in …

commits.messageBody:
…m ref)

Maintainer asked whether images can be stored without introducing a branch
(Actions artifacts? PR-linked? repository-wide?). Ran a live experiment against
this repo (experiments/storage-probe.sh) creating throwaway refs via the Git Data
API and fetching the resulting raw URLs:

- Actions artifacts: cannot be embedded inline (zip archives, auth/expiring URLs,
  workflow-only creation).
- Release assets: require a tag, pollute Releases, unreliable inline on private.
- Gists: cross-account, poor binary support.
- Git tag: blob/<tag>/<path>?raw=true -> HTTP 200 image/png (verified).
- Custom ref refs/hive-mind-media/* via commit-SHA blob/<sha>/<path>?raw=true ->
  HTTP 200 image/png (verified) -- invisible in all GitHub UI lists.

Documents findings in case study (research-notes.md Finding 6, README). No code
change yet; storage-ref choice (custom ref vs tag vs branch) left for maintainer.

…tudy

Adds Finding 6 to research-notes.md and a summary bullet to the case-study
README, recording the live-experiment results that the PR comment references:
GitHub Actions artifacts cannot be embedded inline; release assets need a tag and
pollute Releases; gists are cross-account; a git tag and a custom ref namespace
(refs/hive-mind-media/*) via the commit-SHA ?raw=true URL both serve HTTP 200
image/png. Also commits the experiment evidence logs (force-added past
.gitignore).
